techniques.evidence_quote: NOBELIUM abusing identities and credentialed access to maintain persistence and facilitate covert access
the IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS SSO), enables customers to use ADFS as an identity source (an external identity provider (IdP))
NOBELIUM compromised your ADFS server with MagicWeb
masquerading as one of your valid users to perform further malicious activity and persist indefinitely
a possible NOBELIUM actor using credentials obtained for an ADFS user through MagicWeb
Review the identity provider metadata and SAML certificates
ADFS users assumed the AWS SSO roles both current and historic ... the use of the API call AssumeRoleWithSAML
manually review what activity might be malicious ... all activity performed by the ADFS users and any roles assumed by these users
this actor logged into AWS via the ADFS IdP and blocked all future executions of a Lambda function, causing a DoS for the business

attack_chain.description: NOBELIUM deployed the MagicWeb backdoor on the victim's ADFS server, implanting a malicious DLL that allows the attacker to authenticate as any user by manipulating SAML token claims/assertions without needing valid credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: NOBELIUM compromised your ADFS server with MagicWeb

attack_chain.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
attack_chain.description: NOBELIUM used MagicWeb-forged SAML assertions to impersonate a valid ADFS user, generating fraudulent credentials accepted by the AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO) SAML integration without triggering normal authentication controls.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a possible NOBELIUM actor using credentials obtained for an ADFS user through MagicWeb

attack_chain.description: The attacker exploited the trusted ADFS-to-AWS SAML federation relationship. By controlling the ADFS IdP via MagicWeb, the attacker could issue SAML assertions that AWS IAM Identity Center trusted unconditionally, enabling lateral movement from the on-premises ADFS environment into AWS.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS SSO), enables customers to use ADFS as an identity source (an external identity provider (IdP)). If you have an AWS environment with ADFS configured as your IdP, and NOBELIUM compromised your ADFS server with MagicWeb, NOBELIUM may have moved laterally into your AWS environment

attack_chain.description: Once the forged SAML assertion was accepted, the attacker assumed an AWSReservedSSO_ role inside AWS, operating as a legitimate ADFS-federated user with all privileges that user's permission set granted.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: masquerading as one of your valid users to perform further malicious activity and persist indefinitely ... roles typically start with 'AWSReservedSSO_' ... the use of the API call AssumeRoleWithSAML

attack_chain.description: Using the assumed AWS SSO role, the NOBELIUM actor blocked all future executions of a Lambda function, causing a denial-of-service impact to the business over approximately a 20-minute interval.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: this actor logged into AWS via the ADFS IdP and blocked all future executions of a Lambda function, causing a DoS for the business. In addition, our risk engine flagged this activity as a high notification to review.

initial_access_vector: NOBELIUM deployed the MagicWeb backdoor on the victim's on-premises ADFS server, enabling forged SAML assertions that were trusted by AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO), allowing the attacker to authenticate as any valid ADFS-federated AWS user.
persistence_mechanism: MagicWeb DLL implant on the ADFS server allows the attacker to forge SAML assertions and authenticate as any user at will, providing indefinite persistent access to federated AWS environments as long as ADFS remains the configured IdP.
privilege_escalation: Attacker assumed AWSReservedSSO_ IAM roles via AssumeRoleWithSAML using forged SAML assertions, inheriting the full permission set of the impersonated user without needing the user's actual credentials.
